filename: Adding a chemical which already exists in unit to chemical inventory.docx
relative_path: Waste management/CHEMICAL INVENTORY HANDLING INSTRUCTIONS OCT.2018/Adding a chemical which already exists in unit to chemical inventory.docx
full_text:
Adding a chemical which already exists in unit to chemical inventory

Choose Search > Simple Search. Type to CAS Registry# field CAS number of a chemical or name of the chemical to Substance name field. (It is advisable to use CAS number). Click Search. If you get a result: ”Sorry, your query retuned no results”, please move to another set of instructions.

If you get results, click Container ID field of container which has the correct CAS number.

Choose Create > Copy Container.

In Location ID choose Browse and browse to desired.

Fill at least all fields marked with red. If you use your own abbreviation, take off the selection ”Autogenerate Container ID” and type identification code.

In Number of Copies type the number of copies you want to create.

Click OK.

In Window with text “New Container has been created” click again OK.

If you added multiple containers and use own abbreviation, you need to edit identification code for every added container (Update Edit container).
